title: Polystylus cornu-cervi
intro: Polystylus cornu-cervi, commonly known as the antlered polystylus or deer antler polystylus, is a medicinal plant used in traditional Chinese medicine. It has been valued for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiphlogistic properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Polystylus cornu-cervi include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Polystylus cornu-cervi is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powder, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Polystylus cornu-cervi is classified within the family Compositae, which is now known as Asteraceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, including its flower structure and leaf arrangement. The genus Polystylus is a part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. More specifically, Polystylus cornu-cervi belongs to the order Asterales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes various types of daisies and sunflowers. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
